binaries.title: ndiswan.sys - PPP Multilink receive return-value / frame-ownership refactor (no reachable vulnerability)

changed_functions.name: DoMultilinkProcessing
key_changes: Return type changed from __int64 (returning 0x103 NDIS_STATUS_SUCCESS or 0xC0000001 STATUS_UNSUCCESSFUL) to void
Removed the return-code writes: 'mov eax, 103h' (at 0x1C002C44E) and 'mov eax, 0C0000001h' (at 0x1C002BF10) are eliminated; all exits fall through to a common epilogue at 0x1C002C458

The two internal NdisWanFreeRecvDesc call sites are unchanged: reject/alloc-failure free (unpatched 0x1C002C1D9, patched 0x1C002C1E3) and realloc-and-replace free of the old descriptor (unpatched 0x1C002C213, patched 0x1C002C21D)
Reorder-queue insertion, allocation (NdisWanAllocateRecvDesc), and free logic are otherwise identical; block reordering shifts jump targets by a few bytes
changed_functions.security_notes: MP frame reassembly handler. Parses the MP sequence number and inserts the received-frame descriptor into a sorted reorder list, replaces it with a freshly allocated copy, or rejects/frees it. There are exactly two internal free sites. In the unpatched build every path that frees the descriptor internally returns 0x103, and the only non-0x103 return (0xC0000001, early length rejection at 0x1C002BF10) does not free the descriptor. The return value therefore carried no state that could induce a double-free. The claimed additional free sites at 0x1C002C087 and 0x1C002C131 do not exist; those addresses are counter-increment / WPP-trace blocks for out-of-window detection that converge on the single free at 0x1C002C1D9.

changed_functions.name: ReceivePPP
key_changes: After 'call DoMultilinkProcessing', the unpatched build propagates the return value via 'jmp loc_1C002D8D3' -> 'mov ebx, eax' (0x1C002D8D3); the patched build executes 'mov ebx, 103h' (0x1C002D95A) then 'jmp loc_1C002D8D5', hardcoding NDIS_STATUS_SUCCESS for the MP path
Unpatched call to DoMultilinkProcessing is at 0x1C002D954; patched call is at 0x1C002D955
Non-MP PPP path (ProcessPPPFrame, 0x1C002D488) still propagates its return value unchanged
An rcx/rdx swap for the frame-data pointer is a cosmetic register-allocation difference
changed_functions.security_notes: PPP receive handler. For MP frames it calls DoMultilinkProcessing; for non-MP PPP frames it calls ProcessPPPFrame. The upstream dispatcher ProtoCoReceiveNetBufferListChain (0x1C0008A60) calls the handler via the guarded indirect call on the connection pointer at +0x78 and frees the descriptor with NdisWanFreeRecvDesc only when the returned status is not 0x103. By forcing 0x103 on the MP path, the patch makes DoMultilinkProcessing the sole owner of MP frames so the dispatcher never frees them. This is a defensive ownership refactor, not a fix for a reachable double-free.

security_findings.entry_point: PPP frame reception via the NDIS WAN protocol receive path (ProtoCoReceiveNetBufferListChain, 0x1C0008A60)
call_chain: ProtoCoReceiveNetBufferListChain (0x1C0008A60): guarded indirect call on connection pointer +0x78, single free at 0x1C0008EE4 when status != 0x103
ReceivePPP (0x1C002D820): PPP receive handler, routes MP frames to reassembly
DoMultilinkProcessing (0x1C002BE9C): MP reassembly / reorder queue; two internal free sites (0x1C002C1D9, 0x1C002C213 unpatched)
security_findings.description: The patch changes DoMultilinkProcessing from returning an NTSTATUS-style code to void and makes its sole caller ReceivePPP return a hardcoded 0x103 (NDIS_STATUS_SUCCESS) for the multilink path instead of propagating the reassembly result. This transfers sole ownership of the received-frame descriptor to DoMultilinkProcessing and prevents the upstream dispatcher from freeing multilink frames. The originally suspected double-free is not reachable in the unpatched build: the free/return contract is self-consistent. Every path in DoMultilinkProcessing that frees the descriptor internally (0x1C002C1D9 reject/alloc-failure, 0x1C002C213 realloc-and-replace) reaches 'mov eax, 103h' at 0x1C002C44E and returns 0x103, so the dispatcher does not free again. The only non-0x103 return is 0xC0000001 at 0x1C002BF10 (early length rejection), which does not free the descriptor, so the dispatcher's follow-up free is the descriptor's single release. Paths that retain the descriptor in the reorder list also return 0x103. Consequently the descriptor is released exactly once on every path and no double-free (or leak) is reachable in the unpatched build. The change is a robustness/hardening simplification of the ownership contract, not the repair of a present, reachable defect.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None. No code path frees the descriptor and then reports a status that induces a second free. The two internal free sites both return 0x103, and the dispatcher frees only on non-0x103 statuses, which never free internally.
security_findings.primitive: None. No double-free or use-after-free primitive is present. The previously described pool-spray / reallocation / arbitrary-write / token-stealing chain is not supported by the binaries and does not apply.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED ReceivePPP (0x1C002D820) - MP dispatch propagates status:
0x1C002D951 : mov     rdx, rbx          ; rdx = frame descriptor
0x1C002D954 : call    DoMultilinkProcessing
0x1C002D959 : jmp     loc_1C002D8D3     ; -> 'mov ebx, eax'
0x1C002D8D3 : mov     ebx, eax          ; propagate reassembly return

PATCHED ReceivePPP (0x1C002D820) - MP dispatch forces success:
0x1C002D952 : mov     rcx, rdi
0x1C002D955 : call    DoMultilinkProcessing   ; now void
0x1C002D95A : mov     ebx, 103h               ; always NDIS_STATUS_SUCCESS
0x1C002D95F : jmp     loc_1C002D8D5           ; epilogue

UNPATCHED DoMultilinkProcessing (0x1C002BE9C) - free sites and returns:
0x1C002BF10 : mov     eax, 0C0000001h   ; only non-0x103 return (early length reject)
0x1C002BF15 : jmp     loc_1C002C453     ; epilogue, frame NOT freed
0x1C002C1D6 : mov     rcx, r14          ; frame descriptor (reject/alloc-fail convergence)
0x1C002C1D9 : call    NdisWanFreeRecvDesc
0x1C002C1DE : jmp     loc_1C002C44E     ; -> 'mov eax, 103h'
0x1C002C1F7 : call    memmove
0x1C002C204 : mov     rcx, r14          ; OLD frame descriptor
0x1C002C213 : call    NdisWanFreeRecvDesc
0x1C002C222 : mov     r14, [rsp+98h+arg_18]  ; NEW descriptor inserted
0x1C002C44E : mov     eax, 103h         ; shared success return
0x1C002C463 : retn

PATCHED DoMultilinkProcessing (0x1C002BE9C) - void epilogue:
0x1C002BF1C : jmp     loc_1C002C458     ; early reject, no 'mov eax'
0x1C002C458 : add     rsp, 58h          ; common epilogue, eax undefined
0x1C002C468 : retn

DISPATCHER ProtoCoReceiveNetBufferListChain (0x1C0008A60) - single free site:
0x1C0008E98 : mov     rax, [rsi+78h]                 ; handler pointer (ReceivePPP)
0x1C0008E9C : call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; guarded indirect call
0x1C0008EA2 : cmp     eax, 103h
0x1C0008EA7 : jz      loc_1C0008EE9                  ; consumed -> skip free
0x1C0008EE1 : mov     rcx, rbx                        ; frame descriptor
0x1C0008EE4 : call    NdisWanFreeRecvDesc             ; single free (non-0x103 only)

breakpoints.why: PPP receive handler. Confirms MP detection (protocol 0x3D short / 0x00 0x3D long, connection flag +0x124 bit 4) and the return value forced to 0x103 in the patched build.

MP reassembly. Confirms which of the two internal free sites (0x1C002C1D9, 0x1C002C213) is taken and that these paths reach the 0x103 success return; confirms the only non-0x103 return (0x1C002BF10) does not free.
Frame free routine. Log rcx (frame descriptor) at entry to verify empirically that no descriptor is passed twice across an internal free and the dispatcher's follow-up free.
Dispatcher's single NdisWanFreeRecvDesc call, reached only when the handler returned a status other than 0x103 (i.e. an early-reject frame that was not freed internally).

watch: rcx at NdisWanFreeRecvDesc entry - frame descriptor being freed; on the unpatched build no descriptor appears twice
eax after 'call DoMultilinkProcessing' in unpatched ReceivePPP - 0x103 on freed/retained paths, 0xC0000001 only on early length reject (not freed)
frame descriptor +0x38 bit 0 - selects free method (NdisFreeNetBufferList vs NdisFreeNetBufferListContext + NdisReturnNetBufferLists)
frame descriptor +0x58 - header pointer freed via ExFreeToNPagedLookasideList inside NdisWanFreeRecvDesc
key_offsets: 0x1C002D8D3: 'mov ebx, eax' - propagates reassembly return in unpatched ReceivePPP
0x1C002BF10: 'mov eax, 0C0000001h' - only non-0x103 return in unpatched DoMultilinkProcessing (no free)
0x1C002C44E: 'mov eax, 103h' - shared success return reached by both internal free sites
0x1C002C213: 'call NdisWanFreeRecvDesc' - realloc-and-replace free of old descriptor (unpatched)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable - no reachable vulnerability. The change is a return-value/ownership refactor and there is no double-free trigger.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: No fault. On the unpatched build the frame descriptor is released exactly once on every path (internally when the status is 0x103, or by the dispatcher when the status is non-0x103 and no internal free occurred).
debugging_aids.notes: Frame descriptor offsets: +0x38 flags (bit 0 = driver-allocated NBL), +0x40 PPP protocol type, +0x48 data pointer, +0x50 data length, +0x58 header pointer (freed via ExFreeToNPagedLookasideList), +0x70 NET_BUFFER_LIST pointer. Connection context (arg1): +0x50 link/bundle context, +0x78 framing receive-handler pointer (ReceivePPP for PPP), +0x124 protocol flags (bit 4 = MP enabled), +0x264 error counter. Link/bundle context (*(arg1+0x50)): +0x40 window size, +0x48 MP options (bit 5 = short sequence, bit 6 = multiclass), +0xF8 sequence window mask, +0xFC sequence window validity mask, +0x66C error counter. Addresses are RVAs from image base 0x1C000000; both builds share the same base.

summary: The patch changes the return-value contract between two PPP receive functions in ndiswan.sys. DoMultilinkProcessing (0x1C002BE9C, the Multilink Protocol reassembly routine) is changed from returning an NTSTATUS-style code (0x103 success / 0xC0000001 failure) to void, and its sole caller ReceivePPP (0x1C002D820) now returns a hardcoded 0x103 (NDIS_STATUS_SUCCESS) for the multilink path instead of propagating the reassembly result. This makes DoMultilinkProcessing the sole owner of the received-frame descriptor and stops the upstream dispatcher ProtoCoReceiveNetBufferListChain (0x1C0008A60) from freeing multilink frames. The originally suspected double-free (CWE-415) is NOT reachable in the unpatched build: the free/return contract is self-consistent. Both internal free sites (0x1C002C1D9, 0x1C002C213) return 0x103, so the dispatcher does not free again; the only non-0x103 return (0xC0000001 at 0x1C002BF10, an early length rejection) does not free the descriptor. The dispatcher frees only on non-0x103 statuses, so the descriptor is released exactly once on every path. The finding is downgraded to no reachable vulnerability; the change is a defensive ownership-contract refactor. An independent function-by-function comparison confirms only these two functions changed and no other security-relevant change was omitted.
